content.body: The human skeleton has 206 bones. Its functions are: SUPPORT (holds body upright), PROTECTION (skull protects brain, ribs protect heart/lungs), MOVEMENT (with muscles), BLOOD CELL PRODUCTION (red marrow), MINERAL STORAGE (calcium, phosphorus).

TYPES OF BONE:
• Long bones: femur, humerus (levers for movement)
• Short bones: wrist, ankle (shock absorption)
• Flat bones: skull, ribs, shoulder blade (protection)
• Irregular bones: vertebrae

JOINTS: Where two or more bones meet.
• Fixed/immovable: skull sutures
• Cartilaginous: vertebrae (slightly movable)
• Synovial (freely movable): shoulder (ball-and-socket), knee (hinge), wrist (condyloid)
Synovial joint features: cartilage (smooth surface), synovial fluid (lubrication), synovial membrane (produces fluid), ligaments (bone-to-bone), tendons (muscle-to-bone)

questions.question: How many bones does the adult human skeleton have?
questions.explanation: The adult human skeleton has 206 bones. Babies have more (about 270) — some fuse together as we grow.

questions.question: Which part of the skeleton protects the brain?
questions.explanation: The skull is made of flat bones fused together to form a protective case around the brain.

questions.question: Ligaments connect:
questions.explanation: Ligaments are tough, slightly elastic fibres that connect bones at joints.

questions.question: Tendons connect:
questions.explanation: Tendons are strong, inelastic cords that attach muscles to bones.

questions.question: The knee joint is a ___ joint.
questions.explanation: Hinge joints allow movement in one plane only (like a door hinge). Knee, elbow, ankle.

questions.question: Ball-and-socket joints allow:
questions.explanation: Ball-and-socket (shoulder, hip) allows flexion, extension, rotation, and circumduction.

questions.question: Synovial fluid in joints functions to:
options: Lubricate joint surfaces and reduce friction
questions.answer: Lubricate joint surfaces and reduce friction
questions.explanation: Synovial fluid is a viscous liquid that lubricates cartilage surfaces, preventing wear.

questions.question: Red blood cells are produced in:
questions.explanation: Red bone marrow (in sternum, vertebrae, pelvis) produces red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets.

questions.question: Cartilage covers bone ends in synovial joints to:
questions.explanation: Articular cartilage is smooth and slightly compressible — reduces friction and absorbs shock.

questions.question: Vertebrae in the spinal column are connected by ___ joints.
questions.explanation: Intervertebral discs (cartilage) connect vertebrae — slightly movable, absorb shock.

questions.question: Which is a long bone?
questions.explanation: Long bones (femur, humerus, tibia) act as levers for movement.

questions.question: Osteoporosis is a disease where bones:
options: Become brittle and porous due to calcium loss
questions.answer: Become brittle and porous due to calcium loss
questions.explanation: Osteoporosis: bone density decreases as calcium is lost — bones fracture easily.

questions.question: The shoulder joint is a:
questions.explanation: The shoulder has the greatest range of movement — the rounded head of humerus fits into glenoid cavity.

questions.question: Exoskeleton (like in insects) is found:
options: Surrounding the outside of the body
questions.answer: Surrounding the outside of the body
questions.explanation: Insects, crabs, and other invertebrates have hard exoskeletons outside their bodies for support and protection.

questions.question: Which statement about tendons is TRUE?
questions.explanation: Tendons must transmit force without stretching — they are tough, inelastic collagen fibres.

questions.question: The ribcage protects the:
questions.explanation: 12 pairs of ribs curve around the chest cavity protecting the heart and lungs.

questions.question: Mineral storage in bones mainly involves:
questions.explanation: Bones store ~99% of body's calcium and ~85% of phosphorus, released when blood levels drop.

questions.question: A sprain is damage to:
questions.explanation: Sprain = stretched/torn ligament. Strain = stretched/torn muscle or tendon.

questions.question: The vertebral column consists of how many vertebrae?
questions.explanation: 33 vertebrae: 7 cervical, 12 thoracic, 5 lumbar, 5 sacral (fused), 4 coccygeal (fused).

questions.question: Hydrostatic skeleton (fluid pressure) is found in:
questions.explanation: Earthworms use fluid pressure in their body cavity (coelom) as a skeleton — circular + longitudinal muscles work against it.
